mission.title: Install llm-d Predicted Latency Scheduling
mission.description: Deploy the experimental predicted latency based load balancing feature for llm-d. The Endpoint Picker (EPP) integrates with in-pod latency predictor sidecars that continuously learn from live traffic, estimating p90 TTFT and TPOT for each candidate pod. An SLO scorer compares predictions against per-request SLOs for intelligent routing. Requires building custom EPP and sidecar images from an experimental branch.

steps.title: Install the Inference Gateway Extension
steps.description:
Follow the official Gateway API Inference Extension installation:
https://gateway-api-inference-extension.sigs.k8s.io/guides/

Ensure you have Docker/BuildKit and access to a container registry.

steps.title: Build the custom EPP image
steps.description:
Clone and build the EPP from the experimental branch:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api-inference-extension.git
cd gateway-api-inference-extension
git checkout slo-prediction-experimental

export IMG="<your-registry>/epp:slo-prediction-$(git rev-parse --short HEAD)"
docker build -t "$IMG" -f Dockerfile .
docker push "$IMG"
```

steps.title: Build the latency predictor sidecars
steps.description:
Build the training and prediction sidecar images from the same experimental branch:
```bash
# See: https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api-inference-extension/tree/slo-prediction-experimental/latencypredictor-v1
```

Build and push training server and prediction server images to your registry.

steps.title: Configure the plugins ConfigMap
steps.description:
Create the `plugins-config` ConfigMap with both `default` and `slo` scheduling profiles:
```yaml
apiVersion: inference.networking.x-k8s.io/v1alpha1
kind: EndpointPickerConfig
plugins:
  - type: queue-scorer
  - type: kv-cache-utilization-scorer
  - type: prefix-cache-scorer
  - type: slo-request-tracker
  - type: slo-scorer
  - type: slo-aware-profile-handler
  - type: max-score-picker

schedulingProfiles:
  - name: default
    plugins:
      - pluginRef: slo-request-tracker
      - pluginRef: prefix-cache-scorer
      - pluginRef: queue-scorer
      - pluginRef: kv-cache-utilization-scorer
      - pluginRef: max-score-picker
  - name: slo
    plugins:
      - pluginRef: prefix-cache-scorer
        weight: 0
      - pluginRef: slo-request-tracker
      - pluginRef: slo-scorer
      - pluginRef: max-score-picker
```

steps.title: Deploy the InferencePool with sidecars
steps.description:
Apply your InferencePool/EPP manifest with:
- EPP container with `--enable-latency-predictor` flag
- Training sidecar (port 8000)
- Three prediction sidecars (ports 8001, 8002, 8003)
- ConfigMaps for `latency-predictor-config` and `prediction-server-config`

See the [example manifest](https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/gateway-api-inference-extension/blob/slo-prediction-experimental/config/manifests/inferencepool-resources-lp.yaml) for reference.

steps.title: Verify the deployment
steps.description:
Check all containers are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods
```

Verify health endpoints:
```bash
# Training sidecar
curl http://<pod-ip>:8000/readyz

# Prediction sidecars
curl http://<pod-ip>:8001/readyz
curl http://<pod-ip>:8002/readyz
curl http://<pod-ip>:8003/readyz
```

steps.title: Test SLO-aware routing
steps.description:
Send requests with SLO headers to trigger predicted latency scheduling:
```bash
curl -v $GW_IP/v1/completions \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -H 'x-prediction-based-scheduling: true' \
  -H 'x-slo-ttft-ms: 200' \
  -H 'x-slo-tpot-ms: 50' \
  -d '{"model": "meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct", "prompt": "Hello", "max_tokens": 200, "stream": "true"}'
```

The final SSE frame includes both predictions and actuals for validation.

resolution.summary: A successful deployment shows all EPP and sidecar containers running. SLO-aware requests return predictions alongside actuals in the response. EPP logs at verbosity -v=4 show profile selection, candidate scoring, and pod picking based on predicted latency.

uninstall.title: Remove the deployment
uninstall.description:
Follow the cleanup instructions from the [Inference Gateway Extension guide](https://gateway-api-inference-extension.sigs.k8s.io/guides/#cleanup).

Delete the InferencePool, ConfigMaps, and supporting resources.

uninstall.title: Clean up container images
uninstall.description: Remove the custom EPP and sidecar images from your container registry if no longer needed.

upgrade.title: Pull latest experimental branch
upgrade.description:
```bash
cd gateway-api-inference-extension
git pull origin slo-prediction-experimental
```

upgrade.title: Rebuild and redeploy images
upgrade.description: Rebuild EPP and sidecar images with updated code, push to registry, and update the deployment manifests with new image tags.

troubleshooting.title: Prediction sidecars not ready
troubleshooting.description:
Check sidecar logs and readiness endpoints:
```bash
kubectl logs <epp-pod> -c prediction-server-1
curl http://<pod-ip>:8001/readyz
```

Prediction sidecars need training data before they can make predictions. Allow time for the training sidecar to collect samples.

troubleshooting.title: Predictions inaccurate initially
troubleshooting.description: The XGBoost models need sufficient training data (minimum 100 samples). Initial predictions may be inaccurate. The training sidecar retrains every second by default (`LATENCY_RETRAINING_INTERVAL_SEC=1`).

troubleshooting.title: Scaling limits at high QPS
troubleshooting.description: Each prediction sidecar sustains approximately 300 QPS. Total prediction load grows with both cluster QPS and pod count. Scale prediction servers horizontally if needed.

troubleshooting.title: Only streaming mode supported
troubleshooting.description: The predictor requires `"stream": "true"` in the request body. Non-streaming requests will not produce training data or predictions.

prerequisites.description: A Kubernetes cluster with GPU support, the Gateway API Inference Extension installed, Docker/BuildKit for building custom images, and access to a container registry. Only streaming workloads are supported.
